source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/02_ADD (HAD1417MCW)/▶ CE MDD/■ 7. ADD(HAD1417MCW) Dimdi 등록관련 기술문서 제출 221104/Post Market Surveillance Report [Doc.No.A-PMS-004].docx

document.title: Post Market Surveillance Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector
document.revisionOrVersion: Report No.: A-TCF-5-000 (Rev.1); Revision History includes No. 2, 1st update
document.documentDate: Jan/29/2021; Revision History No. 2 date Dec/07/2021

sourceSummary.oneLine: Post-market surveillance report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector documenting PMS sources, adverse event searches, FSCA/FSN handling, and PMCF assessment.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Intended for authorized competent authorities and notified bodies of H&abyz Co. Ltd. as PMS evidence supporting the PMS Plan.
keyPoints: ADD is identified as Abyz Digital Detector, a Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector for general radiographic diagnosis of adult and children anatomy, not for mammography.
The device is classified as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
PMS activities used literature reviews, media searches, and experience with similar devices based on PMS sources listed in NB-MED-2.12 / 1.
Searches in Korea, Europe, and USA found no adverse event or recall cases related to similar Digital Flat Panel X-ray detectors.
The PMS result states no additional measures such as product design change or process improvement are required.
The PMCF assessment concludes PMCF does not need to be performed.

concepts.name: Post Market Surveillance
concepts.evidence: This Post Market Surveillance (PMS) has been conducted in accordance with the PMS Plan (Doc. No. A-PMS-003).

concepts.name: Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector
concepts.evidence: ADD(Abyz Digital Detector), Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Class IIa
concepts.evidence: Classification Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.

concepts.name: Field Safety Corrective Action
concepts.evidence: In the event of serious adverse events and an accident with our product, Field Safety Corrective Action will be implemented in accordance with MEDDEV 2.12 Post-Market Surveillance.

concepts.name: Field Safety Notice
concepts.evidence: H&abyz will include a copy of the Field Safety Notice (FSN) Notice submitted to the competent authority.

concepts.name: Post-Market Clinical Follow-up
concepts.evidence: But all the cases are not met to PMS activity data. So, the PMCF does not need to be performed.

evidence.claim: The document is a Post Market Surveillance Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Post Market Surveillance Report for ADD Abyz Digital Detector

evidence.claim: The report identifies A-TCF-5-000 Rev.1 as the report number and revision.
The report was prepared, reviewed, and approved on Jan/29/2021.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared Sales team No, Jungmin Jan/29/2021; Review R&D Manager Lee, Dong jin Jan/29/2021; Approval Quality Manager Hur, Namkyu Jan/29/2021

evidence.claim: The revision history records an initial release on June/28/2020, an FSN/FSCA plan addition on Jan/29/2021, and a first update on Dec/07/2021.
evidence.evidenceText: 0 Initial Release June/28/2020; 1 Add to plan of FSN/FSCA Jan/29/2021; 2 1st update Dec/07/2021

evidence.claim: The manufacturer is H&abyz Co., Ltd. in Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacture H&abyz Co., Ltd. 1F, 2-dong, 41-16, cheoinseong-ro, Namsa-myeon, Cheoin-gu, Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea

evidence.claim: The product name is Abyz Digital Detector and the model name is ADD.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Name Abyz Digital Detector Model Name ADD

evidence.claim: ADD is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of adult and children anatomy and is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-Ray Detector is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children... Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The device is classified as Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX under MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification Class IIa by Rule 16 of Annex IX, MDD 93/42/EEC as amended by Directive 2007/47/EEC.

evidence.claim: PMS activities were conducted according to the PMS Plan and selected literature reviews, media, and experience with similar devices from NB-MED-2.12 / 1 PMS sources.
evidence.evidenceText: The PMS Annual Analysis activities were conducted based on the PMS Plan’s Chapter 5. Source of PMS Information. To collect data, we selected three sources from the PMS sources listed in 'NB-MED-2.12 / 1'.

evidence.claim: The literature search date was 2021.01.05 to 2021.11.30 and covered publications from 2000 to 2019.
evidence.evidenceText: Date of Search 2021.01.05 ~ 2021.11.30... Period Covered by Search From 2000 to 2019 years

evidence.claim: The literature sources were PUBMED, European Radiology, and Medical Physics.
evidence.evidenceText: Literature Source PUBMED, European Radiology, Medical Physics

evidence.claim: Media searches for digital detector related keywords in Google and Yahoo found no applicable results.
evidence.evidenceText: We searched as keyword such as ‘Solid State X-Ray Imager’, ‘Digital Imager’, ‘Flat panel digital X-ray detector’, ‘digital detector’, ‘Flat panel detector’, ‘detector’, ‘X-ray detector’, ‘FPD’ in google and yahoo, it is not found applicable

evidence.claim: Korea, Europe, and USA similar-device adverse event searches were performed on 2021.11.26.
The Korea search found no adverse event or recall related to Digital Flat Panel X-ray detectors.
evidence.evidenceText: There is no adverse event or recall related to the Digital Flat Panel X-ray detector in the Korea
evidence.pageTarget: Korea medical device adverse event search

evidence.claim: The Europe and USA searches did not identify recall or accident report cases for similar equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: Conclusion - As a result of confirming the accident report of similar equipment, we have not encountered any case related to recall and accident report.

evidence.claim: No additional measures such as product design change or process improvement were required after post-2020.06 market research analysis.
evidence.evidenceText: As a result of the post-2020.06 market research analysis, no additional measures such as product design change and process improvement are required.

evidence.claim: For serious adverse events and accidents, FSCA will be implemented according to MEDDEV 2.12 Post-Market Surveillance.
evidence.evidenceText: In the event of serious adverse events and an accident with our product, Field Safety Corrective Action will be implemented in accordance with MEDDEV 2.12 Post-Market Surveillance.

evidence.claim: H&abyz will include a copy of the FSN submitted to the competent authority and proceed before and at the same time as FSCA starts.
evidence.evidenceText: H&abyz will include a copy of the Field Safety Notice (FSN) Notice submitted to the competent authority. It will proceed before and at the same time as the FSCA is started.

evidence.claim: The PMCF assessment concluded PMCF does not need to be performed.
evidence.evidenceText: But all the cases are not met to PMS activity data. So, the PMCF does not need to be performed.

reviewFlags.reason: Source metadata authoritySignal is MFDS/domestic, but the source text primarily supports CE MDD, competent authorities/notified bodies, and Korea/Europe/USA database searches.
Document identifier is ambiguous: sourcePath references Doc.No.A-PMS-004, while the document body states Report No.: A-TCF-5-000 (Rev.1) and references PMS Plan Doc. No. A-PMS-003.
Revision metadata is internally inconsistent or unclear: cover states Rev.1 with Jan/29/2021 signatures, while revision history includes No. 2, 1st update, dated Dec/07/2021.
Product metadata is broader than the document body: productSignal lists HAD/A/F 1417-1717, but the source text identifies only Product Name Abyz Digital Detector and Model Name ADD.
PMCF table row 3 contains wording inconsistent with the identified X-ray detector device: 'Our device is a product that pushes the limbs and helps the blood to circulate well.'
